data.title: US drillers keep oil and natgas rig count unchanged for second week in a row, says Baker Hughes
data.description: U.S. energy firms this week kept the number of oil and natural gas rigs unchanged for a second week in a row, energy services firm Baker Hughes said in its closely followed report on Friday.

data.title: USDA Launches Inquiry Into Data Collection — Market Talk
data.description: Agriculture secretary Brooke Rollins says that the USDA is looking into its data collection process and reporting, in an effort to improve accuracy and market trust. "We want to make sure at the USDA that we're framing the status of the market," says Rollins. To that end, the USDA is starting its formal request for information, seeking comment from market participants. Rollins tells attendees at the USDA Agricultural Outlook Forum that making these improvements is a focus for the USDA over the course of the next year. The agricultural market has expressed concern about the accuracy of USDA reporting amid the larger cuts to government operations made by the Trump administration. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: SES: 2025 EBITDA rose 5% to CAD 501M; 2026 guidance is CAD 520–550M with a 5% dividend hike
data.description:
Adjusted EBITDA grew 5% year-over-year to CAD 501 million in 2025, with strong cash flow and shareholder returns despite commodity volatility. 2026 guidance is CAD 520–550 million, supported by contracted infrastructure and a 5% dividend increase. Metal recycling headwinds are expected to ease as logistics normalize.

data.title: Trade Uncertainty in Canada to Remain Until USMCA Resolved — Market Talk
data.description: The Supreme Court ruling on President Trump's global tariffs will have little to no impact on the near-term path for Canadian interest rates, say strategists at TD Securities. TD says the USMCA review remains the key focus for the Bank of Canada and fixed-income traders. The firm estimates roughly 90% of Canadian exports to U.S. were exempt from Trump's Ieepa tariffs because they complied with USMCA. The court ruling "does little to reduce the ongoing uncertainty that has weighed on [Canadian] business investment for the last year," TD says. Firms have told the BOC that uncertainty over USMCA's future has put investment and hiring plans on hold. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Phillips 66 SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Phillips 66, a diversified energy manufacturing and logistics company, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's performance across its various segments, including Midstream, Chemicals, Refining, Marketing and Specialties (M&S), and Renewable Fuels, as well as outlining future strategic priorities and addressing key challenges and risks.

Financial Highlights

Sales and other operating revenues: Decreased 8% in 2025, primarily due to lower prices for crude oil, refined petroleum products, and NGL, partially offset by higher sales volumes.

Net income attributable to Phillips 66: $4,403 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, compared with $2,117 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. The increase was primarily due to a before-tax aggregate gain of $1.9 billion associated with the partial sale of Germany and Austria Marketing and improved realized refining margins.

Income before income taxes: $5,420 million in 2025, compared with $2,675 million in 2024. The increase was driven by gains from asset sales and improved refining margins.

Income tax expense: $892 million in 2025, compared with $500 million in 2024, primarily due to higher income before income taxes.

Net cash provided by operating activities: $4,962 million in 2025, a $0.8 billion increase compared with 2024, primarily due to higher earnings driven by improved realized refining margins.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through several segments, including Midstream, Chemicals, Refining, Marketing and Specialties (M&S), and Renewable Fuels. The Midstream segment focuses on crude oil and refined petroleum product transportation, terminaling, and processing services. The Chemicals segment involves a 50% interest in Chevron Phillips Chemical Company LLC, producing petrochemicals. The Refining segment processes crude oil into petroleum products. The M&S segment markets refined products and includes the manufacturing of base oils and lubricants. The Renewable Fuels segment processes renewable feedstocks into renewable products.

Geographical Performance: The company operates refineries in the United States and Europe, with significant activities in the Central Corridor region following the acquisition of the remaining 50% interest in WRB Refining LP. The M&S segment has a strong presence in the U.S. and Europe, with notable sales in Germany and Austria.

Sales Units: In 2025, the company reported a worldwide refining crude oil capacity utilization rate of 94% and a clean product yield of 87%. The Midstream segment reported NGL production of 462 thousand barrels daily and NGL pipeline throughput of 917 thousand barrels daily. The Renewable Fuels segment produced 38 thousand barrels daily and sold 66 thousand barrels daily.

New Production Launches: The company completed the Coastal Bend acquisition, enhancing its NGL operations with long-haul pipelines and fractionation facilities. Additionally, the Rodeo Complex is a key site for renewable fuels production.

Future Outlook: The company has set strategic priorities through 2027, focusing on world-class operations, disciplined growth, financial strength, and shareholder returns. It plans to invest in low-capital, high-return projects to enhance refining segment returns and increase utilization rates. The 2026 capital budget is set at $2.4 billion, with significant investments in Midstream and Refining segments to support growth and operational efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Phillips 66 has outlined strategic priorities focusing on world-class operations, disciplined growth and returns, financial strength and flexibility, and shareholder returns. The company is optimizing utilization rates and product yield at refineries, targeting an annual clean product yield of greater than 86% and crude oil capacity utilization rates higher than the industry average. In 2025, the company ceased fuel production at the Los Angeles Refinery and acquired the remaining 50% interest in WRB Refining LP for $1.3 billion to enable full integration with its broader value chain. The company also completed the Coastal Bend acquisition to expand its NGL operations.

Capital Management: In 2025, Phillips 66 generated $5 billion in cash from operations, funded $2.2 billion in capital expenditures and investments, and completed acquisitions totaling $3.5 billion. The company repurchased $1.2 billion of its common stock and paid $1.9 billion in dividends. It issued $2 billion in junior subordinated notes and maintained a total debt-to-capital ratio of 39%. The company also increased its accounts receivable securitization facility to $1.25 billion and utilized $367 million of this capacity by year-end.

Future Outlook: Phillips 66 plans to maintain total annual capital expenditures and investments of approximately $2.5 billion through 2027, focusing on organic growth in its Midstream and Chemicals businesses. The company aims to reduce total debt to $17 billion and lower its debt-to-capital ratio by the end of 2027. It also targets returning more than 50% of net cash provided by operating activities to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. The 2026 capital budget is set at $2.4 billion, with significant investments in Midstream and Refining segments to enhance asset reliability and market capture.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: The company faces significant market risks due to the cyclical and volatile nature of margins for its products, which are influenced by factors beyond its control, such as global demand, geopolitical risks, and technological advances. The company does not produce its own feedstocks, making it vulnerable to fluctuations in crude oil and other feedstock prices, which can impact its financial results.

Regulatory Risks: Regulatory changes, particularly those related to renewable fuels and environmental standards, pose additional risks, potentially increasing operational costs and affecting profitability.

Operational Risks: The company is also exposed to operational risks, including planned and unplanned downtime, business interruptions, and hazards inherent in its operations, such as explosions and environmental incidents. Reliance on third-party transportation for crude oil and other products introduces risks of supply interruptions and increased costs.

Joint Ventures: Joint ventures decrease the company's ability to manage risks, as partners may have conflicting interests or fail to meet obligations.

Public Health Crises: Public health crises, such as pandemics, could materially impact the company's operations and financial results.

Management Strategies: The company is focused on optimizing operations and cost structures to enhance returns, particularly in its Refining segment. Strategic priorities include disciplined growth in Midstream and Chemicals, financial strength through debt reduction, and shareholder returns via dividends and share repurchases. The company is also addressing risks related to climate change and regulatory pressures by investing in renewable fuels and technologies. Management is aware of the potential impacts of geopolitical developments and economic volatility on its operations and is taking steps to mitigate these risks through strategic planning and capital allocation.

SEC Filing: Phillips 66 [ PSX ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Discontinuance of Counterclaims Against D2 Lithium Corp. Totaling $550,000 Completed
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Langley, British Columbia – TheNewswire - February 20, 2026 - D2 Lithium Corp.(“D2 Lithium or the Company”) (TSX-V: DWTO) (OTC: DTWOF) wishes to report thediscontinuance of five counterclaims filed against D2 Lithium in theCourt of King's Bench of Alberta in September 2023 for a total of$550,000 by former directors, officers and certain employees of theCompany.  Full and final releases in favour of D2 Lithium have beenreceived from all of the former directors, officers and employees thatwere plaintiffs in the counterclaims.

D2 Lithium also wishes to provide an update on the litigation claimsagainst various parties including the Company’s former directors,Chris Brown and Sameer Uplenchwar, in Court File Number 2301-04232,and their spouses, Tara Brown and Nandita Apte, in Court File Number2401-06205.  The multiple litigation claims have now beenconsolidated into a single action, Court File Number 2301-04232. Details of these claims were the subject of prior news releasesdated July
2, 2024 and March 31,
2023.  A copy of all of the filings is available tothe public from the Court of King’s Bench in Calgary, Alberta.  TheCompany will provide further updates on material developments as theybecome available.

data.title: Consumer-Discretionary Sector Could See Meaningful Upside Thanks to Tariff Ruling — Market Talk
data.description: The Supreme Court ruling that strike down President Trump's global tariffs is "a clear positive for consumer discretionary with high import exposure, easing cost pressures and margin drag," Jefferies analysts say in a research note. While benefits will likely be widely felt, the analysts point to Signet Jewelers, Yeti, SharkNinja, Nike and Birkenstock as companies with the largest potential upside. Each company has faced a weighted-average tariff impact ranging from 6.9% up to 15.1%, and each will be positioned for meaningfully higher earnings with that pressure removed, Jefferies says. Signet, SharkNinja and Birkenstock are higher in midday trading, while Yeti and Nike are lower. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Google Expands Financing Efforts To Boost AI Chip Sales In Bid To Rival Nvidia: Report
data.description:
Alphabet Inc.’s (GOOG) Google is reportedly trying out new financial plays as it seeks to increase market adoption of its artificial intelligence chips, in a bid to compete with Nvidia Corp. (NVDA).

According to a report from the Wall Street Journal, which cited people familiar with the matter, Google has amped up financial support to many of its data center partners that provide computing power to customers, in a move to expand its market potential.

Google is also reportedly mulling an investment of about $100 million in cloud-computing company Fluidstack, as part of a deal that values the startup at around $7.5 billion.

As per the report, Google is also speaking with other data center partners about increasing its financial commitments, hoping for additional TPU demand.

Shares of GOOG were up 4% at the time of writing. 

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Nurses in New York reach tentative deal to end six-week strike
data.description:
By Joseph Ax

The last remaining group of striking nurses in New York City has reached a tentative agreement with their hospital, the nurses' labor union said on Friday, potentially ending what the union called the largest work stoppage for nurses in city history after six weeks.

Approximately 4,200 nurses at NewYork-Presbyterian will vote this weekend on a new contract that would increase salaries by more than 12% over three years, protect health benefits and provide new safeguards for the use of artificial intelligence, according to the New York State Nurses Association.

The strike began on January 12, when 15,000 nurses at three major health systems walked out, seeking better pay and safer working conditions. The work stoppage caused staffing shortages and prompted New York Governor Kathy Hochul to declare a state of emergency to allow out-of-state and foreign workers to cover for striking nurses.

Two-thirds of the nurses returned to work on February 14 after reaching agreements with Montefiore and Mount Sinai hospitals.

"For a month-and-a-half, through some of the harshest weather this city has seen in years, nurses at NYP showed this city that they won't make any compromises to patient care," Nancy Hagans, the union president, said in announcing the NewYork-Presbyterian deal.

In a statement, a NewYork-Presbyterian spokesperson, Angela Karafazli, said, "We are pleased to have reached a tentative settlement with NYSNA, through the mediator, that reflects our tremendous respect for our nurses."

data.title: Jakks Pacific Shares Surge on Narrowed 4Q Loss
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Jakks Pacific rose after the company narrowed its loss in the fourth quarter.

Shares surged 22% to $22.15 as of Friday afternoon. The stock has fallen 36% over the past year.

The toy maker late Thursday reported a quarterly loss of $5.32 million, or 47 cents a share, compared with a loss of $9.11 million, or 83 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company posted a loss of 18 cents a share.

Sales fell 2.8% to $127.1 million, down from $130.7 million a year earlier.

In the company's dolls, role-play and dress-up segment, sales fell 22% to $48.8 million. Outdoor and seasonal-toys revenue meanwhile jumped 54% to $13 million and action-play and collectibles sales rose 19% to $56.2 million.

Sales in the costumes division fell 28% to $9.1 million.

Chief Executive Officer Stephen Berman said demand began to normalize in the fourth quarter following significant disruptions in the second and third quarters from changes in U.S. tariff policy.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Tumbles 12% — Analyst Flags Execution Risks Ahead Of Earnings
data.description:
Key points:
  • Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss cited operational challenges in a constrained supply environment.
  • The firm said it would look for several improvements, including expanding active power capacity beyond 850 megawatts, and resolving past data center construction delays before turning constructive.
  • CoreWeave is scheduled to report fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results on February 26.

CoreWeave Inc. (CRWV) shares slid on Friday after Morgan Stanley’s initiation cast a cautious tone just days before the company’s quarterly earnings release. 

According to a Barron’s report, the firm assigned the cloud infrastructure provider an “Equal Weight” rating and set a $99 price target, suggesting only modest upside from recent levels.

Execution In Focus

Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss cited operational challenges in a constrained supply environment as a primary reason for the tempered outlook. He pointed to difficulties in fulfilling customer demand and noted that some dissatisfied clients have pursued legal action.

The firm acknowledged that CoreWeave has landed and broadened significant agreements with some of the most sophisticated generative AI customers in the market. However, the firm stressed that investor confidence hinges on tangible execution milestones.

CoreWeave stock tumbled over 12% by Friday afternoon. On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around the stock remained in ‘bearish’ territory amid ‘low’ message volume levels. 

Morgan Stanley indicated it would look for several improvements before turning more constructive. Among them are expanding active power capacity beyond 850 megawatts, resolving past data center construction delays, converting development projects into realized capital spending, and diversifying its customer base.

Additional Pressure Points

Compounding the negative sentiment, a Business Insider report said that Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) was unable to secure financing for a proposed data center transaction involving CoreWeave, creating fresh uncertainty around expansion efforts.

CoreWeave is scheduled to report fourth-quarter (Q4) fiscal 2025 results on February 26. Analysts expect revenue of approximately $1.53 billion, and an adjusted loss per share of $0.49, according to Fiscal AI data. 

CRWV stock has gained over 114% in the last 12 months.
